subject: [PATCH 4/5] fuse: update file mode when updating acls

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary:
Reviewer suggested rephrasing a comment block to clarify that fuse userspace is responsible for updating access permissions in the inode only for non-local fuse servers, and proposed restructuring the logic of fuse_do_setattr() to track whether the kernel updated mode bits separately.

Reviewer noted that the logic in fuse_do_setattr() is not clear and suggested reordering the checks to make it more obvious when kernel or userspace updates mode bits, also pointing out that for local/exclusive servers, the kernel already sets refreshed attributes so the invalidation logic can be skipped.

2026-03-25.patch_summary: This patch updates the fuse kernel module to correctly handle file mode changes when updating ACLs. It introduces a boolean flag to track whether the kernel updated the mode bits, making the code more readable and clear in its intent. The patch also restructures the logic for setting attributes on the inode, simplifying the process and reducing unnecessary operations.
